paragraphs.text: A plausible chemistry reason is that quantitative metal–ligand stability measurements in scCO₂ are quite rare, because ionic complexes are often poorly soluble unless specially fluorinated ligands or modifiers are used. If you want, I can next look for the closest related SRD-46 data, such as **non-aqueous CO₂-related media**, **fluorous ligands**, or **mixed-solvent systems**.

grounded_claims.evidence_snippet: General chemistry: ionic species have low solubility in neat/supercritical CO2; not recorded in SRD-46 stability rows.
Well-known: fluorinated ligands/modifiers enhance solubility in CO2; not captured in SRD-46 stability constant tables.
